title: Debug: Fix Missing ConfigMap Key
description:
A Deployment is stuck because it references a ConfigMap key that does not exist — a common real-world misconfiguration. The pod will show `CreateContainerConfigError`. Your job: apply the broken manifest, read the error, identify the wrong key name in `configMapKeyRef`, and fix it.

**Common Pitfalls:** Key names in ConfigMaps are case-sensitive. `db_host` and `DB_HOST` are entirely different keys. Always match the key name in `configMapKeyRef.key` exactly to what is defined in the ConfigMap's `data` section.
steps: Apply the manifest as-is and observe the pod status with kubectl get pods
Run kubectl describe pod on the failing pod to read the error event
Inspect the ConfigMap with kubectl get configmap app-settings -o yaml to see the actual keys
Identify the mismatch between the key name in configMapKeyRef and the ConfigMap's data keys
Fix the key name in the env.valueFrom.configMapKeyRef section and re-apply
Verify the pod reaches Running state
hints: kubectl describe pod will show an event like: 'couldn't find key X in ConfigMap'
Compare the key in configMapKeyRef.key against the actual keys in the ConfigMap's data section
ConfigMap key names are case-sensitive — even a capital letter difference causes failure
After fixing, kubectl apply -f and then kubectl rollout status deployment/broken-app

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: app-settings
data:
  DATABASE_URL: "postgres://db:5432/myapp"
  LOG_LEVEL: "warn"
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: broken-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: broken-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: broken-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: busybox
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo DB=$DATABASE_URL && sleep 3600']
        env:
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: app-settings
              key: database_url  # TODO: This key name is wrong — fix it to match the ConfigMap

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: app-settings
data:
  DATABASE_URL: "postgres://db:5432/myapp"
  LOG_LEVEL: "warn"
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: broken-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: broken-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: broken-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: busybox
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo DB=$DATABASE_URL && sleep 3600']
        env:
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: app-settings
              key: DATABASE_URL
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo 'Checking ConfigMap app-settings exists...'
if ! kubectl get configmap app-settings &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ ConfigMap app-settings not found'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ ConfigMap app-settings exists'

echo 'Checking DATABASE_URL key exists in ConfigMap...'
DB_URL=$(kubectl get configmap app-settings -o jsonpath='{.data.DATABASE_URL}')
if [ -z "$DB_URL" ]; then
  echo '✗ DATABASE_URL key missing from ConfigMap app-settings'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ DATABASE_URL key present in ConfigMap'

echo 'Checking Deployment broken-app exists...'
if ! kubectl get deployment broken-app &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ Deployment broken-app not found'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ Deployment broken-app exists'

echo 'Waiting for Deployment to be available...'
if ! kubectl rollout status deployment/broken-app --timeout=90s; then
  echo '✗ Deployment broken-app did not become available — pod may have CreateContainerConfigError'
  kubectl describe pod -l app=broken-app | tail -20
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ Deployment broken-app is available'

echo 'Verifying env var DATABASE_URL is set correctly inside pod...'
POD=$(kubectl get pod -l app=broken-app -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
ENV_VAL=$(kubectl exec "$POD" -- sh -c 'echo $DATABASE_URL' 2>/dev/null)
if [ -z "$ENV_VAL" ]; then
  echo '✗ DATABASE_URL env var is empty inside pod'
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ DATABASE_URL is set inside pod: $ENV_VAL"

echo ''
echo 'All checks passed!'
exit 0
